notes: Reproduced severe performance degradation in SKCanvasView raster backend when drawing scaled images in Blazor WASM. Drawing a 786x1090 image at 1:1 takes <1ms, but scaling to 1600x1600 or using canvas.Scale(2) takes ~38ms per frame (42x slowdown). The degradation is proportional to output pixel count. Main branch (Debug build, net8.0) shows ~50ms for all modes with no scaling variance, but this is likely due to Debug overhead masking the scaling cost rather than a fix. Root cause is CPU-based image scaling in the raster backend.

reproductionSteps.description: Create Blazor WASM project with SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor 3.119.0 and WasmBuildNative=true
reproductionSteps.command: dotnet new blazorwasm -n Repro --framework net10.0 && cd Repro && dotnet add package SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor --version 3.119.0
reproductionSteps.output: Restore succeeded. PackageReference for package 'SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor' version '3.119.0' added.

filesCreated.description: Blazor page with SKCanvasView benchmark: creates 786x1090 test image, measures per-frame DrawImage timing across 4 modes (1:1, scaled-800, scaled-1600, scale-2x) with 20 measured frames per mode

reproductionSteps.output: Build succeeded with 2 warning(s) in 16.0s (CS0618 obsolete API warnings only)

Mode: scaled-800     Avg:    51.05ms  Min:    50.00ms  Max:    54.00ms  Frames: 19  EstFPS: 19.6

versionResults.notes: 1:1=0.89ms, scaled-800=9.62ms (10x slower), scaled-1600=38.06ms (42x slower), scale-2x=37.53ms (42x slower)
1:1=0.67ms, scaled-800=9.53ms (14x slower), scaled-1600=37.89ms (56x slower), scale-2x=36.75ms (55x slower). Same scaling degradation as 3.119.0.

All modes ~50ms with no scaling variance. However, this is a Debug build on net8.0 (vs Release NuGet on net10.0), so the ~50ms baseline overhead likely masks the scaling cost. Result is inconclusive for fix verification.

corrections.corrected: Reporter uses SkiaSharp 3.119.0 on Blazor WASM. TFM not specified in issue, tested with net10.0 which is forward-compatible.

actionability.reason: Confirmed severe performance degradation (42x) in SKCanvasView raster backend when scaling images in WASM. Reproduces on latest stable 3.119.2. The root cause is architectural (CPU-based image scaling) but may be improvable through JS interop optimizations.

actions.description: Apply platform and area labels confirmed by reproduction

workarounds: Pre-scale the image at load time to the target display size, then draw at 1:1 in the paint callback to avoid per-frame CPU scaling
Use SKGLView instead of SKCanvasView for image-heavy rendering (if DrawLine issues can be worked around)
missingInfo: What specific issues does the reporter experience with SKGLView and DrawLine calls? A separate issue would help track that.
